creator: roelof@nisser.com
text:
Peter Pentchev wrote:
> 
> ...
> Are you sure you have the latest devel/libtool port installed?
> I seem to remember *very* recent fixes making it install libltdl.

I've switched tactics to just wipe and reinstall. Even so I seem
to have my timing wrong ;). Take libmcrypt. There was a version
mismatch, I reported it and patched it by hand. Something(s) else
went awry, I wiped for luck and started afresh. Guess what...
libmcrypt has a *new* version mismatch ;). Unbelievable. And that's
just one port.

> G'luck,

Thanks, the way this project has been going I'll be needing it <g>.

Roelof

PS needless to say the only external traffic is due to cvsup'd
ports. Updated since the last cvsup, that is ;). Maybe it wouldn't
be a bad idea to somehow mold the ports into CVS as well. So that
there too would be a release, a stable and a blood dripping bleeding
edge current hierarchy. It wouldn't be a panacee of course, but
at least that way you could do dry runs and expect the results
to be repeatable on the target boxii. I've found out the hard way
that currently that is not the case :). It is making me wonder
how to upgrade boxes in the field. If you can't test and expect
the results to be applicable "later".

In that sense these 'mishaps' of late teach a harsh but valuable
lesson. That even performing dry runs gives no garuantee with the
current setup. E.g. heimdal happily runs what donar and nisse balk
upon. After I had postfix problems with nisse, I pretested most
with heimdal in order to have smooth sailing with donar.

A hard lesson indeed. Still, better now then later when I have some
serious users on the box. Or rather, more of them ;).
